places.name: teamLab Planets
places.name_ja: チームラボプラネッツ
places.description: PLACEHOLDER — digital art museum in Toyosu. Book tickets ~2 weeks ahead; go barefoot, wear shorts.

places.name: Senso-ji Temple
places.description: PLACEHOLDER — Tokyo's oldest temple, in Asakusa. Go early (before 09:00) to beat the crowds on Nakamise street.

places.name: Placeholder Ramen Bar
places.description: PLACEHOLDER — the ramen place we saved. Ticket machine at the entrance, cash only.

places.name: Placeholder Kissaten Coffee
places.description: PLACEHOLDER — old-school Japanese coffee house for a slow morning.

places.name: Don Quijote Shibuya
places.description: PLACEHOLDER — chaotic everything-store; good for souvenirs and snacks. Tax-free counter with passport.

places.name: Placeholder Ryokan with Onsen
places.description: PLACEHOLDER — traditional inn, dinner included (kaiseki). Tattoo policy: check private bath option.

places.name: Hakone Open-Air Museum
places.description: PLACEHOLDER — sculptures in the mountains, Picasso pavilion, foot bath.

places.name: Fushimi Inari Shrine
places.description: PLACEHOLDER — the thousand torii gates. Sunrise visit = no crowds; full loop ~2h.

places.name: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove
places.description: PLACEHOLDER — combine with Tenryu-ji temple and the monkey park.

places.name: Placeholder Matcha House
places.description: PLACEHOLDER — matcha everything; expect a queue after 11:00.

places.name: Nishiki Market
places.description: PLACEHOLDER — 'Kyoto's kitchen'. Knives, pickles, street snacks. No eating while walking.

places.name: Dotonbori at night
places.description: PLACEHOLDER — neon canal walk, Glico sign photo.

places.name: Placeholder Okonomiyaki Grill
places.description: PLACEHOLDER — the okonomiyaki spot we bookmarked; also try takoyaki nearby.

tips.body: PLACEHOLDER — Get a Suica/Pasmo in Apple/Google Wallet for all metro and convenience stores.
PLACEHOLDER — Trash cans are rare: carry a small bag for your garbage.
PLACEHOLDER — Hakone Free Pass covers the loop (train, cable car, ropeway, boat).
PLACEHOLDER — Buses get packed; the subway + walking is often faster.
PLACEHOLDER — Stand on the RIGHT on escalators in Osaka (opposite of Tokyo).
PLACEHOLDER — Lockers available; you will walk in water up to your knees.
PLACEHOLDER — Open 24h; sunrise or after 17:00 for photos without crowds.
PLACEHOLDER — Dinner is served early (~18:00); arrive before 17:00.
